_rationale: C++ MCP search now runs the real trigram-backed SearchEngine via handle_search (src/mcp/handlers_core.cpp). The substring-on-symbol-names parity-compat stub in src/cli/mcp.cpp (hardcoded score:855.5, column:1, no regex/flags/output/semantic/ranking) was removed; the real handler attributes each text match to its enclosing symbol via ref_tracker.get_symbol_at_line and emits the Go-shape payload {result_id, object_id, file, line, column, match, score, symbol_type, symbol_name, is_exported}. Both binaries return the same 4-item result set for pattern 'add' on multi-lang ({a.go::Add, b.py::add, c.cpp::add, d.rs::add}) and the same enclosing-symbol attribution (symbol_name/symbol_type per match). Two value divergences remain in contexts[]: (b) C++ enrichment — context_lines emitted with the matched source line; Go omits the field. (d) Ranking — both binaries compute their own match scores (Go ~855.5/125.5, C++ ~121.5) and item order; same multiset, different ordering. Both are masked below with rationale; the rest of the body (item count, file paths, lines, match strings, symbol attribution) is locked C++-side by HandlersFixture.Search* in tests/mcp_handlers_core_test.cpp.

tiers._rationale_stable: JSON-RPC envelope and content type. result.content[].text carries (b) context_lines enrichment and (d) score/order divergence so it is held in the ignore tier — see _rationale_ignore. Structural body parity (item count = 4, file set = {a.go,b.py,c.cpp,d.rs}, match strings = {Add,add,add,add}, symbol attribution) is locked C++-side by the HandlersFixture.Search* unit tests.
tiers._rationale_ignore: result.content[].text diverges on two sub-paths only. (b) Intentional C++ enrichment — handle_search emits results[].context_lines (the matched source line) when context is available; Go's MCP search omits the field. Same accepted-divergence class as cli/symbols/inspect.signature. (d) Ranking divergence — Go and C++ compute different score scales (Go ~855.5/125.5; C++ ~121.5 from a different ranker) and emit results in different orders; same multiset of matches, different position. Tracked under A38Q2RR8ZcyL (ranking parity convergence). The centralized inner-text ignore list (tests/parity/runner/parity_runner.cpp::normalize_mcp_inner_text) already masks results[].object_id, results[].result_id, results[].file_id, results[].column, results[].is_exported so those don't need per-descriptor masks. Standard JSON-RPC envelope noise (id, jsonrpc, result.meta.elapsed_ms, result.meta.server_pid) is handled by the centralized envelope ignore baseline.
